# pfsense_system_advanced_notifications (Resource)

[System advanced notifications](https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/config/advanced/notifications.html) configuration including SMTP, Telegram, Pushover, Slack, certificate expiration, and sound settings. This is a **singleton** resource — only one instance can exist per pfSense installation.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "pfsense_system_advanced_notifications" "example" {
  # Certificate Expiration
  cert_enable_notify         = true
  revoked_cert_ignore_notify = false
  cert_expire_days           = 27

  # SMTP (Email) Notifications
  disable_smtp        = false
  smtp_ipaddress      = "mail.example.com"
  smtp_port           = 587
  smtp_timeout        = 20
  smtp_ssl            = true
  smtp_ssl_validate   = true
  smtp_from_address   = "pfsense@example.com"
  smtp_notify_address = "admin@example.com"
  smtp_username       = "pfsense@example.com"
  smtp_password       = "smtp-password"
  smtp_auth_mechanism = "PLAIN"

  # Sound
  console_bell = true
  disable_beep = false

  # Telegram
  telegram_enabled = false

  # Pushover
  pushover_enabled  = false
  pushover_sound    = "devicedefault"
  pushover_priority = "0"
  pushover_retry    = 60
  pushover_expire   = 300

  # Slack
  slack_enabled = false
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `apply` (Boolean) Apply change, defaults to `true`.
- `cert_enable_notify` (Boolean) Enable daily notifications of expired and soon-to-expire certificates. When enabled, the firewall checks CA and certificate expiration times daily.
- `cert_expire_days` (Number) Number of days before certificate expiration to start generating notifications. Default is 27 days.
- `console_bell` (Boolean) Enable the console bell. When enabled, emergency console messages will trigger a bell in connected consoles including serial terminals.
- `disable_beep` (Boolean) Disable the startup/shutdown beep sound through the built-in PC speaker.
- `disable_smtp` (Boolean) Disable SMTP notifications but preserve the settings. Some other mechanisms, such as packages, may need the SMTP settings in place to function.
- `pushover_api_key` (String, Sensitive) Pushover application API key for authentication.
- `pushover_enable` (Boolean) Enable Pushover notifications. Requires a Pushover API key and user key.
- `pushover_expire` (Number) Pushover emergency priority notification expiration time in seconds. Maximum 10800 seconds (3 hours). Default is 300 seconds.
- `pushover_priority` (String) Pushover message priority. Options: `-2`, `-1`, `0`, `1`, `2`. Defaults to `0` (Normal Priority).
- `pushover_retry` (Number) Pushover emergency priority notification retry interval in seconds. Minimum 30 seconds. Default is 60 seconds.
- `pushover_sound` (String) Pushover notification sound. Options: `devicedefault`, `pushover`, `bike`, `bugle`, `cashregister`, `classical`, `cosmic`, `falling`, `gamelan`, `incoming`, `intermission`, `magic`, `mechanical`, `pianobar`, `siren`, `spacealarm`, `tugboat`, `alien`, `climb`, `persistent`, `echo`, `updown`, `vibrate`, `none`. Defaults to `devicedefault`.
- `pushover_user_key` (String, Sensitive) Pushover account user key.
- `revoked_cert_ignore_notify` (Boolean) Ignore notifications for revoked certificates. When enabled, the firewall will not check expiration for certificates that have been revoked.
- `slack_api` (String, Sensitive) Slack API key for authentication.
- `slack_channel` (String) Slack channel name to send notifications to. May only contain lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores. Maximum 80 characters.
- `slack_enable` (Boolean) Enable Slack notifications. Requires a Slack API key and channel name.
- `smtp_auth_mechanism` (String) Authentication mechanism used by the SMTP server. Options: `PLAIN`, `LOGIN`. Defaults to `PLAIN`. Most servers work with PLAIN; some servers like Exchange or Office365 might require LOGIN.
- `smtp_from_address` (String) E-mail address that will appear in the From field of notification emails.
- `smtp_ip_address` (String) FQDN or IP address of the SMTP E-Mail server to which notifications will be sent.
- `smtp_notify_email_address` (String) E-mail address to send notification emails to.
- `smtp_password` (String, Sensitive) Password for SMTP authentication.
- `smtp_port` (Number) Port of the SMTP E-Mail server. Typically 25, 587 (submission), or 465 (smtps).
- `smtp_ssl` (Boolean) Enable SMTP over SSL/TLS for secure email delivery.
- `smtp_timeout` (Number) Connection timeout in seconds for the SMTP server. Default is 20 seconds.
- `smtp_username` (String) Username for SMTP authentication.
- `ssl_validate` (Boolean) Validate the SSL/TLS certificate presented by the SMTP server. When disabled, the server certificate will not be validated but encryption will still be used if available.
- `telegram_api` (String, Sensitive) Telegram Bot API key required to authenticate with the Telegram API server.
- `telegram_chat_id` (String) Telegram chat ID (private) or channel @username (public) for sending notifications.
- `telegram_enable` (Boolean) Enable Telegram notifications. Requires a Telegram Bot API key and Chat ID.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
terraform import pfsense_system_advanced_notifications.example notifications
```
